user_title: Head of Change Execution, WPB Private Banking & Wealth Solutions, Asia Pacific

talking_points.headline: HSBC's Google Cloud AI Deal Opens 200+ Use Cases — Change Execution Has Three Simultaneous Tracks
talking_points.why_it_matters: HSBC's largest-ever AI commitment adds three concurrent delivery programmes to Adam's change execution portfolio, requiring immediate sequencing and governance before H2 2026 delivery begins.
bullets: HSBC and Google Cloud announced a multi-year AI partnership (June 17) using Gemini models targeting 200+ use cases; only initiatives estimated at US$100m+ in revenue or efficiency gains are prioritised for delivery

Three initial programmes land in parallel: hyper-personalised wealth management advice for APAC clients, financial crime detection at 2x speed across ~1 billion monthly transactions, and AI tools for frontline relationship managers
Change Execution must define sequencing and governance now — three simultaneous delivery tracks risk competing for the same APAC programme capacity in H2 2026

source_links.title: HSBC & Google Cloud AI Partnership — HSBC

talking_points.headline: StanChart's AI-Driven 7,000 Job Cuts Signal Competitor Operational Transformation Is Live
talking_points.why_it_matters: Standard Chartered has moved AI-driven workforce transformation from strategy to execution, directly benchmarking the pace HSBC APAC change delivery must match on operational efficiency.
bullets: Bill Winters, CEO of Standard Chartered announced 7,000+ job cuts (15% of corporate roles) by 2030, driven by AI automating back-office functions across Chennai, Bengaluru, Kuala Lumpur, and Warsaw
The move targets an 18% return on tangible equity by 2030 — a 6-point improvement on 2025 — setting a concrete efficiency benchmark for APAC operations that HSBC must respond to
HSBC's change execution roadmap must articulate a comparable AI-enabled efficiency narrative or risk appearing behind peers on both delivery ambition and operational transformation

talking_points.headline: HSBC Life: Two-Thirds of HK/China HNWIs Have No Legacy Plan — APAC Product Gap Is Open
talking_points.why_it_matters: HSBC's own survey quantifies a major unaddressed legacy planning gap in Adam's APAC markets, creating a concrete cross-sell and product delivery opportunity across Private Banking and Insurance.
bullets: An HSBC Life survey of 203 HNWIs (published June 22) found two-thirds in Hong Kong and mainland China have no legacy plan; market volatility — not life events — is the primary trigger (47-51% of respondents)
Life insurance is the dominant planning tool for 80-92% of respondents, revealing a clear cross-sell opportunity between HSBC Life and HSBC Private Banking that is not yet fully captured across APAC

Converting this insight into a coordinated advisory and product campaign requires WPB Change Execution to align Insurance, Private Banking, and Premier Banking delivery — a natural fit for Adam's mandate

source_links.title: HNWIs Lack Legacy Plans — The Standard HK

HSBC News.title: HSBC and Google Cloud Announce Multi-Year AI Partnership Targeting 200+ Use Cases
HSBC News.summary: HSBC and Google Cloud launched a multi-year AI partnership using Gemini models to enable 200+ use cases focused on hyper-personalised wealth management advice, 2x-faster financial crime detection, and RM tools—each initiative requiring US$100m+ projected value to proceed.

HSBC News.title: Two-Thirds of HNWIs in HK and China Lack Legacy Plans, HSBC Life Survey
HSBC News.summary: HSBC Life's June 22 survey of 203 HNWIs across Hong Kong and mainland China finds two-thirds have no legacy plan, with market volatility as the primary trigger; life insurance is the top planning tool for 80-92% of respondents.

Competitor Intelligence.title: Standard Chartered Plans 7,000+ Job Cuts by 2030 as AI Automates Back-Office
Competitor Intelligence.summary: Standard Chartered announced 7,000+ job cuts (15% of corporate roles) by 2030 driven by AI automation of back-office functions in Chennai, Bengaluru, Kuala Lumpur, and Warsaw, targeting an 18% return on tangible equity.

Private Banking & Wealth.title: PwC: APAC AWM to Reach US$34.5 Trillion by 2030, Singapore Leads Tokenised Wealth
Private Banking & Wealth.summary: PwC's 2026 APAC AWM report projects AuM reaching US$34.5 trillion by 2030 at a 6.8% CAGR, with tokenised fund AuM growing 41% annually to $715 billion by 2030 and Singapore positioned as the strategic cross-border wealth hub.

AI & Technology.title: FIS and Anthropic Deploy Agentic AI Agent to Compress Banking AML Investigations Days to Minutes
AI & Technology.summary: FIS and Anthropic launched a Financial Crimes AI Agent automating AML alert and case investigations from days to minutes, with BMO and Amalgamated Bank as first users and broader H2 2026 rollout planned for credit decisioning, onboarding, and fraud prevention.
